topIssues.title: On-device inference: enforce every model optimization (fused QJL/TBQ attention, TurboQuant, MTP, KV-quant) — close runtime gaps vs the Eliza-1 contract
topIssues.author: lalalune
topIssues.body:
## Summary

On-device inference speed, performance, and memory overhead are first-class product
constraints. We ship a full optimization stack for Eliza-1 — **TurboQuant** weights,
**QJL** K-cache, **PolarQuant / TBQ3** V-cache, **TCQ** trellis quant, **MTP**
speculative decoding, and **fused QJL/Polar attention kernels** — and the
`plugins/plugin-local-inference/native/AGENTS.md` §3 contract says every applicable
optimization is **mandatory, never skipped**.

A runtime audit (Pixel 9a / Mali-G715, plus code trace of the CPU/Vulkan/Metal/CUDA
paths) found that **several of these optimizations are implemented but not actually
applied on the on-device path** — the model is leaving large, already-built wins on
the table. This is a tracking issue to **enforce that every model uses every
optimization it can**, on every backend, and that **any model that can be quantized
and isn't, is**.

Goal: for each (model tier × optimization × backend), the optimization is either
applied or there is a written, verified reason it cannot be — with host-side
numerical verification and on-device perf/overhead numbers recorded.

---

## Current-state audit — the gaps found

### 1. 🔴 Fused QJL/TBQ attention op is implemented + Mali-safe but **never routed** (highest leverage)

`GGML_OP_FUSED_ATTN_QJL_TBQ` is fully implemented end-to-end — node constructor in
`ggml.c`, CPU forward, Vulkan/Metal/CUDA kernels, `supports_op` gate, tests + benches —
and its type contract exactly matches `eliza-1-0_8b`'s KV design (`typeK: qjl1_256`,
`typeV: tbq3_0`). The fused Vulkan kernels are **Mali-safe** (single workgroup of 32,
`barrier()` + shared-mem tree reductions, **zero subgroup intrinsics**, ~1152 B shared
mem) and are **structurally immune to the generic-FA split-K corruption**.

But the graph builder **never constructs it**: `src/llama-graph.cpp:1996-2004`
dequantizes QJL/TBQ/Polar K/V back to F32→F16 and calls generic `ggml_flash_attn_ext`.
So on every backend we run a generic FA over a **dequantized F16 KV cache** — paying a
full double-cast of the KV cache every step and discarding the entire point of the
QJL/Polar quantization.

Routing is **not** a one-line wire — there are **three hard blockers** (full trace in
the audit; the op is referenced only from tests/benches, never from `src/` or the FFI):

- **(dominant, correctness) Q-projection / per-layer Π.** The op consumes a *256-dim
  QJL sketch* Q (`q->ne[0]==256`, `ggml.c:5706`), i.e. `Π·q` with the **same per-layer
  Π that quantized K**. The graph today produces a 128-dim Hadamard-rotated Q (a
  *different* transform), and the K-quant type-trait path uses a single **process-global
  `seed=42` default Π** (`quants-qjl.c:115-135`) while production QJL needs **per-layer Π
  from the training sidecar** (`packages/training/scripts/quantization/qjl_apply.py`).
  So even wiring Q-projection with the default Π would be numerically wrong vs the
  trained KV. Must determine what Π the shipped `eliza-1-0_8b` GGUF was quantized with
  and plumb it into both K-quant and Q-projection.
- **(correctness) non-causal.** The constructor hardcodes `causal=0` (`ggml.c:5725-5733`)
  and the CPU forward has *no* mask logic (sums all `n_kv` tokens). A decoder LLM
  requires causal masking. The Vulkan shader honors a causal push-constant
  (`fused_attn_qjl_tbq.comp:253`) but it's never set. No mask tensor / sinks / ALiBi /
  soft-cap inputs either (Qwen3 needs causal + GQA — GQA *is* supported).
- **(scope) decode-shaped.** The op dispatches one workgroup per `(q_head, q_pos)` and
  `supports_op` forces `ne[2]==ne[3]==1`, so a 1500-token **prefill = 1500 serial
  dispatches** — worse than batched FA on compute-bound Mali. The fused op is the right
  win for **decode** (correctness + parallelism + KV memory + no double-cast), **not**
  for prefill (see #5).
- There is also **no public QJL-K + Polar-V ggml op** — only `qjl_tbq` (which matches
  eliza-1-0_8b's TBQ3-V). The polar Vulkan pipeline is an orphan (no op/constructor/CPU
  forward). eliza-1-0_8b doesn't need it; larger tiers may.

**Win when done:** for the **decode** path — removes the double-cast + F16 KV
materialization, uses the Mali-safe single-pass kernel (never had the split-K bug), and
unlocks quantized KV (#2). Prefill speed is a separate axis (#5). Anchors:
`ggml-vulkan.cpp:775-777,5245-5248,13946-14005,16952-16963`, `ggml.c:5704-5733`,
`fused-attn-qjl-tbq.c`, `ggml.h:2539`, `src/llama-graph.cpp:1990-2008`.

### 2. 🟠 Bionic host runs an F16 KV cache, not the designed QJL/TBQ/Polar cache

The Android Mali GPU path (`eliza_inference_llm_stream_open`,
`tools/omnivoice/src/eliza-inference-ffi.cpp:2879-2884`) maps `cfg.cache_type_k/v` to a
`ggml_type` but the JNI callers `memset` the config to zero → **F16 KV**
(`elizavoice-jni.cpp:1283-1290` `nativeLlmSelfTest`, `:1157-1165` `nativeLlmStreamOpen`).
So the eliza-1 quantized KV cache (QJL-K / TBQ3-V, the memory + bandwidth win) is **not
used on device**.

**Dependency:** do **not** flip `cfg.cache_type` to `qjl1_256/tbq3_0` yet — without #1
it is a *regression* (CPU-offloaded dequant casts + a missing TBQ3_0 V hop). Sequence:
land #1 first, then set the cache types here.

### 3. 🟠 MTP speculative decoding not wired on the bionic host path

`ELIZA_1_LOAD_METADATA` (mobile-device-bridge) declares `mtp: {draftMin:1, draftMax:2}`
for the MTP tiers, but the bionic host builds `cfg` with `draft_min/max = 0` → **MTP
off**. Per AGENTS.md §3, MTP is mandatory where a NextN head exists (2B+). Wire the draft
window through the JNI/`cfg` to the bionic host so on-device decode uses speculative
decoding. (0.8B is the documented non-MTP tier — exempt.)

### 4. 🔴 Generic FA scalar kernel is **non-deterministic** on Mali (intermittent corruption)

Verified on-device: the same prompt under greedy decode (temp=0) produces *different*
outputs run-to-run — ~50% degenerate into `" His!!!!"` token repetition. Identical
deterministic inputs → different outputs = a **race / uninitialized shared memory** in
the Mali FA scalar path (`flash_attn.comp`), **not** split-K (it persists with
`split_k=1`). Current mitigation: FA disabled on Android (reliable but no speedup, since
Mali has no coopmat). Candidate hardening (from the split-K trace) worth testing as the
actual race fix: init `m_max` to `-FLT_MAX` (`uintBitsToFloat(0xFF7FFFFFu)`) not `-inf`,
remove `[[unroll]]` on the runtime-`k_num` loops, clamp `exp()` args with
`max(m-m_max, -87.0)`, and fix the y over-dispatch to `CEIL_DIV(HSV, subgroup_size)`.
**#1 (fused kernel) sidesteps this entirely** for the eliza-1 KV types.

### 5. 🟡 Prefill is matmul-bound on Mali (no cooperative-matrix)

FA-on prefill ≈ FA-off prefill (~22 s / 1500 tokens) → the bottleneck is the
**scalar mul_mat** (QKV proj + FFN), not attention. Mali-G715 exposes no
`VK_KHR_cooperative_matrix`, so the matmul runs the scalar path. Investigate a
Mali-tuned `mul_mm` (warp-tile, f16-accumulate) and/or evaluate the GGML **OpenCL/Adreno
backend** lineage for Mali. This is the ceiling on on-device prefill latency.

### 6. 🟡 User-downloaded (non-Eliza-1) models — apply every applicable optimization

Eliza-1 tiers ship pre-quantized (TurboQuant Q4 + QJL + Polar). Arbitrary
HuggingFace GGUFs the user installs should still get **every applicable** optimization:
quantize-if-unquantized (don't run F16/F32 weights when a quant works on the device),
KV-cache quantization, flash attention (where the backend's FA is correct), and MTP when
the model has a NextN/draft head. "Any model that can be quantized and isn't, should be."

---

## Optimization × tier × backend audit matrix (to fill in and keep green)

Per tier (`0_8b`, `2b`, `4b`, `9b`, `27b`, `27b-256k`) and backend
(CPU, Vulkan/Mali, Vulkan/desktop, Metal, CUDA), confirm each is **applied at runtime**
(not just present in the bundle):

- [ ] **TurboQuant** weights (turbo3/turbo4) — kernels actually dispatched at runtime
- [ ] **TCQ** (turbo3_tcq) on desktop/pro/server + long-ctx tiers
- [ ] **QJL** K-cache (and the fused QJL attention path, not the dequant hop)
- [ ] **PolarQuant / TBQ3** V-cache (and its fused path)
- [ ] **MTP** speculative decoding (2B+; wired through every host path incl. bionic)
- [ ] **Fused attention kernel** routed (no QJL→F16 dequant hop)
- [ ] **Flash attention** correct + enabled where the backend's FA is verified
- [ ] **KV-cache quantization** active on device (not silent F16 fallback)
- [ ] Weight-quant for user-installed models (no F16/F32 when a quant fits)

A cell is only ✅ when verified by the host harness and/or an on-device run with numbers.

---

## Proposed sequence

1. **Resolve the fused-op blockers** (#1): determine the shipped GGUF's Π (per-layer vs
   default), add causal masking to the fused op, and add a QJL-K+Polar-V op *or*
   standardize V on TBQ3_0. Validate bit-exact vs the host golden reference.
2. **Route the graph to the fused op** (#1) gated on `supports_op` + `cparams.flash_attn`
   so CPU/Vulkan/Metal/CUDA each pick fused-vs-generic correctly.
3. **Enable quantized KV on the bionic host** (#2) once #1 lands.
4. **Wire MTP through the bionic host** (#3).
5. **Fix or retire the generic Mali FA scalar race** (#4) — needed for non-fused KV
   types / non-Eliza-1 models; keep the `split_k=1` ARM gate + FA-disabled fallback until
   then.
6. **Mali matmul / prefill optimization** (#5).
7. **Generalize to user models** (#6): an "optimize on install" pass.

## Verification (no phone required for most of it)

- Host-side golden reference + dispatcher already exist for the fused/QJL/Polar/TBQ
  kernels: `plugins/plugin-local-inference/native/reference/` (`qjl_polar_ref.c`,
  `turbo_kernels.c`), fixtures via `gen_fixture.c`, and
  `native/verify/{vulkan_verify.cpp,metal_verify.mm,cuda_verify.cu}`. `vulkan_verify`
  runs on **lavapipe** (software Vulkan) on the Linux host.
- Add a host-side `flash_attn` split-K-vs-single-chunk correctness test (currently
  missing) to reproduce/guard the Mali race class.
- Record per-tier on-device numbers: prefill tok/s, decode tok/s, peak RSS, first-token
  latency, MTP acceptance rate — gate regressions in `eliza-1.manifest.json` `evals`.

## Notes / non-goals

- Upstream `ggml-org/llama.cpp` (fork base b10012, 2026-05-16) has **no Mali FA fix to
  backport** — the Mali correctness PRs (#23241/#22682/#23359) are still open; the
  post-base FA PRs (#24123/#23420) are AMD/coopmat-only. Our `split_k=1`-on-`VK_VENDOR_ID_ARM`
  gate is ahead of upstream — keep it; do not backport for FA correctness.
- The branding rule holds: user-facing strings say "Eliza-1", never the upstream/kernel
  names.

_Filed from an on-device optimization audit (Pixel 9a / Mali-G715). Kernel-level items
reference the `elizaOS/llama.cpp` submodule; runtime/wiring items are in this repo
(`plugins/plugin-local-inference`, `packages/app-core/platforms/android/.../elizavoice-jni`)._

topIssues.title: E2E coverage sweep: real recorded-mock e2e + CI ship-gate for every slash command, shortcut, view, plugin, and route
topIssues.author: lalalune
topIssues.body:
## Summary

elizaOS already has strong, genuinely non-larp e2e machinery — the scenario-runner boots a real `AgentRuntime` over PGLite and exercises real plugin route handlers via its `api` turn kind, a deterministic LLM proxy with strict fixtures gives secret-free runs, a Mockoon-derived external-API mock fleet stands in for real provider calls, and a `external-api-live-drift.yml` nightly re-verifies those mocks against the real APIs. There are even static "ships-without-a-test → CI fails" gates for UI routes/pages and views (`route-coverage.test.ts`, `view-interaction-coverage.test.ts`, `plugin-tui-view-coverage.test.ts`). **What is missing is an umbrella coverage matrix and the equivalent ship-gate for the three surfaces that fall through the cracks: slash commands, pre-LLM shortcuts (#8791, new), and plugin-registered HTTP routes.** This issue is the audit/umbrella that inventories every command, shortcut, view, plugin and route, asserts each has a real recorded-mock e2e, and adds a CI gate that fails when a new one ships uncovered.

## Current state

### What is real and good (credit where due)

- **Scenario-runner is a real e2e harness, not larp.** `packages/scenario-runner/src/executor.ts` boots a real runtime and, for `kind: "api"` turns, stands up a loopback `http.createServer` that dispatches against `runtime.routes` (the actual plugin-registered handlers) and `fetch`es them (`startScenarioApiServer`, ~line 556; api-turn handler ~line 1261). `packages/scenario-runner/test/scenarios/deterministic-generated-app-routes.scenario.ts` is the gold standard: it registers a real `AppRegistryService`, calls real `handleAppsRoutes`/`handleAppPackageRoutes`/`handleViewsRoutes`, and asserts exact registry/catalog/route-ledger state — zero trivial assertions.
- **Secret-free determinism exists.** `packages/test/mocks/helpers/llm-proxy-plugin.ts` (`createDeterministicLlmProxyPlugin`, strict mode via `SCENARIO_LLM_PROXY_STRICT=1`) fails closed when no fixture matches, validates tool-call/schema output, and reports diagnostics. Wired through `runtime-factory.ts` (`shouldUseStrictDeterministicLlmProxy`).
- **Recorded provider mocks derived from real APIs, kept honest.** `packages/test/mocks/environments/*.json` (Mockoon) + the in-process stateful runner (`packages/test/mocks/scripts/start-mocks.ts`) cover ~24 providers; `packages/test/mocks/README.md` documents covered surfaces and remaining gaps; `provider-coverage-contract.test.ts` fails when the table drifts; `.github/workflows/external-api-live-drift.yml` re-runs `*.real.test.ts` against live APIs nightly to detect mock drift. This is exactly the "mock APIs derived from real calls, cached, validated in CI" the product owner asked for — for **external** APIs.
- **PR-lane deterministic e2e runs.** `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` runs `bun run --cwd packages/scenario-runner test:pr:e2e` (17 deterministic scenarios incl. `deterministic-generated-app-routes`, `deterministic-view-switching`, `deterministic-mcp-actions-routes`, `deterministic-workflow-actions-routes`, `deterministic-github-actions-routes`) plus a scenario-catalog completeness check (`packages/scripts/check-scenario-workflow-coverage.mjs`) that fails if any scenario in `packages/test/scenarios` is orphaned from the workflow globs. 701 scenario files exist.
- **Static ship-gates already exist for UI routes + views.** `packages/app/test/route-coverage.test.ts` diffs the canonical route catalog (`buildRouteCatalog`, mirroring `@elizaos/ui` `TAB_PATHS`) against the all-pages click-safe smoke matrix and fails when a new view/page/tile ships without smoke coverage. `packages/app/test/view-interaction-coverage.test.ts` and `packages/agent/src/__tests__/plugin-tui-view-coverage.test.ts` do the same for view interactions / TUI parity. Rich Playwright `ui-smoke` suite (~60 specs incl. `plugin-views-visual.spec.ts`, `orchestrator-gui-workbench.spec.ts`, `view-manager-actual-flow.spec.ts`).

### The gaps (the actual work)

1. **Slash commands: catalog is served, execution is never e2e-tested.** `plugins/plugin-commands` is a *registry only* — it ships `DEFAULT_COMMANDS` (~26 text commands) + a `COMMAND_REGISTRY` provider but **no action handlers** (confirmed in its CLAUDE.md and `src/index.ts`). The connector catalog (`src/connector-catalog.ts`) adds ~15 navigation/client commands. The wire surface is `GET /api/commands` (`packages/agent/src/api/commands-routes.ts`), consumed by `packages/ui/src/chat/useSlashCommandController.ts`. Tests today:
   - `packages/agent/src/api/commands-routes.test.ts` — calls the handler with **mocked** `json`/`error` fns (no real server, no execution).
   - `plugins/plugin-commands/__tests__/parser.test.ts` + `connector-catalog.test.ts` — unit-level; assert a handful of keys (`bash`, `think`), not per-command.
   - **No scenario, no Playwright spec types `/help` / `/settings` / `/model` into chat and asserts the command executes / navigates.** `grep` for any scenario referencing `api/commands` or slash execution returns empty. So most of the ~40 catalog commands have **zero** behavioral coverage of the click-to-effect path. (Pairs with #8790.)
2. **Shortcuts (pre-LLM action shortcuts): proposed, not built.** #8791 introduces a pre-LLM shortcut registry. It does not exist yet, so it has no coverage and no gate. This umbrella must include shortcuts in the matrix + gate the moment #8791 lands.
3. **Plugin HTTP routes: large route-level e2e gap.** ~38 plugins declare `Plugin.routes` (served in prod via `packages/agent/src/api/runtime-plugin-routes.ts` `tryHandleRuntimePluginRoute`; tested generically in `runtime-plugin-routes.test.ts`). Of the plugins that declare routes in their main index, these have **no dedicated route test and no `api`-turn scenario**: `plugin-anthropic-proxy`, `plugin-cli`, `plugin-computeruse`, `plugin-discord-local`, `plugin-facewear`, `plugin-farcaster`, `plugin-github`, `plugin-imessage`, `plugin-music`, `plugin-mysticism`, `plugin-telegram`, `plugin-video`, `plugin-workflow`, `plugin-xr`. (Covered: `plugin-bluebubbles`, `plugin-discord`, `plugin-signal`, `plugin-social-alpha`, `plugin-whatsapp` have `*-routes.test.ts`.) 10 plugins have **no test file at all** (`plugin-action-bench`, `plugin-discord-local`, `plugin-elizamaker`, `plugin-google-meet-cute`, `plugin-local-embedding`, `plugin-mlx`, `plugin-native-shared-types`, `plugin-omnivoice`, `plugin-tee`, `plugin-xmtp`).
4. **No coverage matrix and no ship-gate for commands/shortcuts/routes.** The existing gates cover UI routes/pages/views. There is **no** `check-command-coverage` / `check-shortcut-coverage` / `check-plugin-route-coverage` gate. The advisory `coverage-gate.yml` only measures line coverage on changed files (`COVERAGE_GATE_ENFORCE=0`) — it does not enforce that a new command/route/view has a behavioral e2e.

## Proposed work

Ordered; depends on the sibling "e2e mock infra" issue for any new recorded fixtures.

1. **Build the canonical coverage inventory (source of truth).** A boot-free script (mirror `packages/scripts/check-scenario-workflow-coverage.mjs` style) that enumerates, from real source:
   - slash commands — `DEFAULT_COMMANDS` (`plugins/plugin-commands/src/registry.ts`) ∪ `navigationCommands()`/client commands (`src/connector-catalog.ts`), i.e. the exact set `GET /api/commands` serves;
   - shortcuts — the #8791 registry (once it exists);
   - views — `Plugin.views` declarations resolved by `packages/agent/src/api/views-registry.ts` + `BUILTIN_VIEWS` (reuse the existing view catalog, do not duplicate #8796/#8797/#8798);
   - plugins + routes — every `Plugin.routes` entry across `plugins/*` (path + method).
   Emit `reports/coverage/e2e-matrix.json` + an HTML contact sheet (same shape as the scenario catalog inventory viewer).
2. **Close the command e2e gap (consumes #8790).** Add a `deterministic-slash-commands.scenario.ts` that hits `GET /api/commands` via an `api` turn and, for each `target.kind`, asserts the real effect: `agent` commands route through the message pipeline, `navigate` commands resolve to a real route, `client` commands fire the right effect. Add a Playwright `ui-smoke` spec that types representative slash commands into the composer and asserts navigation/clear/palette via `useSlashCommandController`. Every catalog command must be referenced by at least one of these.
3. **Close the plugin-route e2e gap.** For each route-declaring plugin currently uncovered (§3 list), add an `api`-turn scenario or a `*-routes.test.ts` that boots the handler through `tryHandleRuntimePluginRoute` (real dispatch, real auth gate, real body parsing) with recorded provider mocks where the route calls out. Prioritize plugins with public routes and user-facing surfaces.
4. **Wire shortcuts** into the matrix + a deterministic scenario as soon as #8791 lands.
5. **Add the CI ship-gate.** A `check-e2e-coverage` script + `e2e-coverage-gate.yml` (or a step in `ci.yaml`) that diffs the inventory (§1) against the discovered test/scenario matrix and **fails** when a command, shortcut, view, plugin, or route exists with no real e2e — following the exact precedent of `route-coverage.test.ts` ("a new view/page/tile that ships without smoke coverage fails CI here"). Start advisory for one cycle (like `coverage-gate.yml`), then flip to required once the baseline is green.
6. **Larp-detection pass.** Add a lint rule / review-checklist item so a "test" that asserts only shape (`length > 0`, `kind === 'navigate'`) without exercising the real handler does not count as coverage in the gate — the gate should require a real `api`/`action`/Playwright turn, not a mocked-`json`-fn unit test.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A committed coverage inventory (`reports/coverage/e2e-matrix.json` + viewer) enumerating every slash command, shortcut, view, plugin, and route from real source.
- [ ] Every command in the `GET /api/commands` catalog has a real e2e (scenario `api`/`action` turn or Playwright spec) that exercises its actual effect — not a mocked handler unit test.
- [ ] Every shortcut in the #8791 registry has a deterministic e2e (gated on #8791 landing; tracked as a checklist item until then).
- [ ] Every route-declaring plugin in the §3 uncovered list has a route-level e2e booting the real handler via `tryHandleRuntimePluginRoute`/`api`-turn, with recorded provider mocks for any external calls.
- [ ] All 10 zero-test plugins either gain a real test or are documented as intentionally test-exempt with written justification.
- [ ] Views remain covered by the existing gates (no regression); this issue references #8796/#8797/#8798 rather than re-implementing view e2e.
- [ ] A `check-e2e-coverage` CI gate fails when a new command/shortcut/view/plugin/route ships without a real e2e; advisory for one cycle, then required.
- [ ] New behavioral tests run deterministically in the PR lane (no secrets) via `SCENARIO_USE_LLM_PROXY=1` + the Mockoon mock fleet; any new external fixtures are covered by `external-api-live-drift.yml`.
- [ ] `bun run --cwd packages/scenario-runner test:pr:e2e` and the new gate are green on `develop`.

## Files & areas likely involved

- `plugins/plugin-commands/src/registry.ts`, `src/connector-catalog.ts`, `src/parser.ts` — command source of truth.
- `packages/agent/src/api/commands-routes.ts` + `commands-routes.test.ts` — `GET /api/commands` (currently mocked-only).
- `packages/ui/src/chat/useSlashCommandController.ts`, `slash-menu.ts`, `components/shell/SlashCommandMenu.tsx` — command execution consumers.
- `packages/agent/src/api/runtime-plugin-routes.ts` (`tryHandleRuntimePluginRoute`) + `runtime-plugin-routes.test.ts` — real plugin-route dispatch.
- `packages/agent/src/api/views-registry.ts`, `views-routes.ts`, `builtin-views.ts` — view surface (reference, don't dup).
- `packages/scenario-runner/src/executor.ts` (`startScenarioApiServer`, `api`-turn handler), `runtime-factory.ts`, `test/scenarios/deterministic-generated-app-routes.scenario.ts` (template).
- `packages/test/mocks/` — `helpers/llm-proxy-plugin.ts`, `environments/*.json`, `scripts/start-mocks.ts`, `helpers/provider-coverage.ts`, `README.md`.
- Existing gate precedents to extend: `packages/app/test/route-coverage.test.ts`, `view-interaction-coverage.test.ts`, `ui-smoke-coverage.test.ts`, `packages/agent/src/__tests__/plugin-tui-view-coverage.test.ts`, `packages/scripts/check-scenario-workflow-coverage.mjs`.
- CI: `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`, `scenario-pr.yml`, `test.yml`, `coverage-gate.yml`, `external-api-live-drift.yml`, `live-scenarios.yml`; new `check-e2e-coverage` script under `packages/scripts/`.
- Uncovered route plugins (new tests): `plugin-anthropic-proxy`, `plugin-cli`, `plugin-computeruse`, `plugin-discord-local`, `plugin-facewear`, `plugin-farcaster`, `plugin-github`, `plugin-imessage`, `plugin-music`, `plugin-mysticism`, `plugin-telegram`, `plugin-video`, `plugin-workflow`, `plugin-xr`.

## Open questions

- **Gate scope per surface:** should the gate require coverage of *every* route on a plugin, or one representative happy-path + auth-gate per plugin? (Per-route is exhaustive but high-churn; per-plugin-surface is the cheaper floor.)
- **What counts as "covered" for a command?** One scenario per `target.kind` (agent/navigate/client) vs one per command. Recommend per-command for `agent`-target commands (behavioral) and per-kind for navigate/client (effect is structural).
- **Larp threshold:** do we hard-fail the gate on mocked-`json`-fn unit tests (like the current `commands-routes.test.ts`), or just not count them? Recommend "don't count, don't fail" so existing unit tests stay.
- **Connector command parity:** should Discord/Telegram native-command registration (`plugins/plugin-discord/catalog-commands.ts`, `plugins/plugin-telegram/src/command-registration.ts`) be in-scope for this gate, or owned by #8790? (Currently they have catalog unit tests but no live-render e2e.)
- **Provider-key-gated routes:** for plugins whose routes only work with real credentials and have no mock yet (e.g. some `plugin-farcaster`/`plugin-video` paths), do we require a new Mockoon fixture (blocks on the mock-infra issue) or accept a `external-api-live-drift`-style nightly-only real test?
- **#8791 sequencing:** shortcuts can't be gated until the registry exists — confirm this umbrella stays open across #8791, or splits the shortcut requirement into a follow-up.

topIssues.title: LifeOps split: complete live owner/agent connector and view validation
topIssues.author: lalalune
topIssues.body:
## Background

LifeOps was split from the old mega-plugin surface into `plugin-personal-assistant` plus domain plugins for calendar, scheduling/reminders, goals, inbox, health, blocker/focus, finances, documents, phone/messages, relationships, etc.

A deep local audit found the current static/code-level split is in good shape: stale registered LifeOps action surfaces were removed, personal-assistant owner actions have role gates plus handler-level owner checks, the split plugins build/test in isolation, and the shared scheduled-task architecture is still routed through the personal-assistant runner rather than a second LifeOps scheduler.

The remaining work is not more local grep/testing. It is live, account-backed validation across OWNER and AGENT sides, native devices, and OAuth/provider state.

## Already validated locally

- `plugin-personal-assistant` focused tests/build passed.
- Split plugin focused tests/typechecks passed for relationships, documents, phone, health, goals, calendar, blocker, scheduling, reminders, finances, inbox, and todos.
- Root `bun run verify` passed before the final upstream submodule-only merge.
- Final upstream sync merged latest `origin/develop`; `origin/develop` is an ancestor of the working branch.
- Final submodule-specific validation passed for `plugins/plugin-local-inference` after the upstream `llama.cpp` gitlink change.
- No stale `plugin-lifeops` migration markers were found by static scan.
- Core planned-tool execution and message action filtering both enforce `roleGate`.
- Personal-assistant owner actions now also guard at handler execution time.

## Remaining work

### 1. Provision live test identities

Create and document the actual test identities/devices needed to validate the split end to end:

- OWNER Google account with Calendar and Gmail enabled.
- AGENT Google account or grant state where the agent has non-owner identity.
- OWNER and AGENT identities for Telegram, Discord, Signal, WhatsApp, iMessage, X, and Slack if Slack is part of the deployed LifeOps surface.
- Twilio test number and recipient allowlist for phone/voice flows.
- Native devices or simulators for iOS/macOS/Android flows that cannot be proven in unit tests.
- Health-provider accounts/devices for Apple Health/HealthKit, Google Fit or Health Connect, Fitbit, Oura, Strava, and Withings as applicable.
- Finance/subscription data sources: Gmail billing corpus, CSV fixture, Plaid or equivalent sandbox, PayPal sandbox if supported.
- Browser/password-manager/remote-desktop/travel-provider accounts where those connectors are expected to participate in personal assistant workflows.

### 2. Validate OWNER vs AGENT permission matrix

For every connector/action surface, test these states and record evidence:

- Unauthenticated connector.
- OWNER authenticated and authorized.
- AGENT authenticated but not owner-authorized.
- Expired/revoked grant.
- Missing required scope.
- Multiple grants where owner-side selection must win.
- Planned tool execution path.
- Direct handler invocation path.
- UI-triggered path from the relevant view.

Expected behavior:

- Owner-only actions deny non-owner execution with a clear permission error.
- Handler-level guards match `roleGate` behavior.
- Connector calls return typed `DispatchResult` data, not booleans or swallowed failures.
- Approval-required outbound actions route through the approval policy rather than sending silently.
- Owner-side grants are selected for owner-only operations.

### 3. Validate live connectors

Run live send/read/sync smoke tests for:

- Google Calendar: list, create/update/delete or draft event, availability, scheduling conflict handling, owner-vs-agent grant behavior.
- Gmail/inbox: read/search, label/archive where supported, outbound email approval flow, billing/subscription extraction if used by finances.
- Telegram, Discord, Signal, WhatsApp, iMessage, X, Slack: status/read/send or draft/send paths, unavailable states, revoked auth, and owner-vs-agent behavior.
- Phone/voice/SMS: Twilio env, outbound call/SMS authorization, inbound routing if supported, Android SMS default-role/native bridge behavior where applicable.
- Health: Apple Health/HealthKit permissions, Google Fit/Health Connect permissions, Fitbit/Oura/Strava/Withings sync and unavailable/error paths.
- Blocker/focus: macOS hosts/SelfControl/admin permission flows, iOS Family Controls, Android Usage Access.
- Finances: subscription detection, payment/provider import, CSV/sandbox paths, approval boundaries for sensitive operations.
- Documents/browser/password-manager/remote desktop/travel providers if included in the deployed assistant workflows.

### 4. Validate views and integration flows

Verify each split view renders, navigates, refreshes, and performs its expected actions with both mock and live data:

- Calendar.
- Scheduling/reminders.
- Goals.
- Inbox.
- Health.
- Blocker/focus.
- Finances/subscriptions.
- Documents.
- Relationships.
- Phone/messages.
- Any remaining compatibility `#lifeops` deep links or aliases in `packages/app`.

For each view, capture:

- Empty state.
- Loading state.
- Error/unavailable connector state.
- Populated state.
- Refresh/retry behavior.
- Mobile and desktop layout.
- Navigation from app launcher/sidebar/deep link.
- Agent-created data showing up in the view.
- View action resulting in the correct agent/tool/connector behavior.

### 5. Add durable live QA artifacts

After accounts/devices are available, add or update:

- A documented LifeOps live-validation matrix with account/device prerequisites.
- Repeatable smoke-test instructions for local and CI-adjacent validation.
- Redacted screenshots or logs proving each connector/view state was exercised.
- Any missing scenario-runner or e2e coverage that can be safely automated without real secrets.
- Clear skip behavior for live tests when credentials/devices are absent.

## Acceptance criteria

- All listed connector families have OWNER and AGENT side evidence.
- Every owner-only action has matching denial behavior through planned tools, direct handlers, and UI-triggered flows.
- Every split view has been checked on desktop and mobile with empty, loading, error, and populated states.
- OAuth/native permission failures are explicit and recoverable in the UI and agent responses.
- Live connector failures return typed results and do not silently succeed.
- Approval-gated outbound flows are validated end to end.
- The validation matrix documents exactly which accounts, devices, scopes, env vars, and provider sandboxes were used.
- Any discovered bug gets a linked issue or PR before this issue is closed.

## Notes

The local audit did not identify remaining stale LifeOps cruft in registered plugin surfaces. The known gap is confidence that real-world OAuth, native permissions, side selection, provider-specific failure modes, and UI integration all behave correctly with live accounts and devices.

topPRs.title: feat(app): harden cloud desktop view routing
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Hardens the cloud-backed desktop/chat/view path so Eliza can reliably run live Cerebras chat, route agent navigation into first-party and plugin views, and load dynamic plugin bundles inside the desktop shell.

This PR includes:
- Cerebras live-provider simulation support for the smoke harness (`gpt-oss-120b` small, `zai-glm-4.7` large) without adding local-inference changes.
- Safer app-control `VIEWS` routing so view commands still work, but BTC/crypto/live-data prompts are not stolen by view routing.
- Desktop view-layout routing fixes for bundle-backed views, including calendar/task-coordinator/TinyPlace-style third-party plugin views.
- DynamicViewLoader host external fixes so real plugin bundles can import `@elizaos/ui` and `@elizaos/ui/components` without undefined component crashes.
- Chat overlay/UI hardening: selectable chat text, cleaner composer controls, bounded timers/listeners, and memory-cycling coverage.
- Desktop packaged regression coverage, iOS plist helper hardening, Android cloud-debug/install harness improvements, and scoped third-party plugin discovery support.

TinyPlace itself remains local POC work only; this PR ships the Eliza-side compatibility and QA harness work.

## Validation

Latest rebased branch was pushed after syncing with `origin/develop`.

- `packages/core`: `bun run typecheck`
- `packages/ui`: `bun run typecheck`
- `packages/app`: `bun run typecheck`
- `packages/app-core`: `bun run typecheck`
- `packages/app`: `bun run lint`
- Core focused Vitest: `message-runtime-stage1`, `message-routing-live-regression`, `message.processAttachments` — 125 tests passed
- UI focused Vitest: `ContinuousChatOverlay`, `DynamicViewLoader`, attachments/download/image utilities — 173 tests passed
- App-core focused Vitest: first-run config, live-provider, websocket pending queue, account usage — 32 tests passed
- Agent plugin resolver Vitest — 3 tests passed
- Deterministic UI smoke: task widget, TinyPlace plugin view, notes/calendar split layout — 3 passed
- Memory smoke: chat/view cycling heap/DOM/listeners bounded — 1 passed
- Live Cerebras view switching: calendar, task-coordinator, notes/documents, Spanish calendar — 4 passed
- Live Cerebras chat: response, time, BTC, BTC price handling, website code — 5 passed
- Packaged desktop launch/regression: launch, persistence, settings reset — 3 passed, 2 platform skips

## Notes

- No secrets are committed. The only key-prefix scan hit is the literal provider prefix `csk-` in provider catalog metadata.
- No local-inference additions are included in this commit.
- The full Android e2e wrapper was intentionally not run because it stages local-inference/voice assets; this branch uses the cloud-debug/mobile contract path instead.

topPRs.title: test(e2e): umbrella e2e coverage gate for slash commands, plugin routes, views (#8802)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Closes part of #8802 — the umbrella e2e coverage matrix + CI ship-gate, plus the slash-command and plugin-route e2e gaps it inventories.

## What this adds

### 1. Canonical coverage inventory + ship-gate (`packages/scripts/e2e-coverage/`)
- **`inventory.ts`** enumerates, from real source, every user-triggerable surface: the served slash-command catalog (`getConnectorCommands`), every route-wiring plugin (`discoverRoutePlugins`), zero-test plugins, and the (not-yet-existing) #8791 shortcut registry — cross-checked against a committed manifest.
- **`manifest.ts`** is the curated source of truth (covered/exempt per surface), the same pattern as `route-coverage.test.ts`'s `DIRECT_ROUTE_CASES`.
- **`__tests__/e2e-coverage.test.ts`** is the enforced `bun test` ship-gate; **`check-e2e-coverage.ts`** emits `reports/coverage/e2e-matrix.json` + an HTML contact sheet.
- **Larp-detection (§6):** a `covered` entry only counts when its artifact exists *and* names the real dispatcher (`tryHandleRuntimePluginRoute` / `buildHonoAppForRuntime`). The mocked-`json`-fn `commands-routes.test.ts` is rejected via `LARP_TEST_ARTIFACTS`.

### 2. Slash commands — close the gap
- `commands-routes.real-server.test.ts` — real loopback `http` server over the real `handleCommandsRoutes`, asserting the served catalog **==** `getConnectorCommands("gui")`.
- `deterministic-slash-commands.scenario.ts` — `api`-turn over the real route on the scenario runtime; wired into the PR-lane `--scenario` list + the `scenario-pr-workflow` / `deterministic-action-coverage` meta-gates.
- `slash-commands.spec.ts` — Playwright browser coverage of navigate/client/agent dispatch through `useSlashCommandController`; wired into `scenario-pr.yml`.

### 3. Plugin routes — close the §3 gap
Real route-level e2e booting the production dispatcher for **11** plugins: `computeruse`, `discord-local`, `elizamaker`, `facewear`, `github`, `imessage`, `music`, `mysticism`, `telegram`, `workflow`, `xr`. (`discord-local` + `elizamaker` were also zero-test — they now have their first tests.)

### 4. CI
- E2E ship-gate + matrix report wired into `scenario-pr.yml`'s `scenario-runner-e2e` job.
- Advisory standalone `e2e-coverage-gate.yml` mirrors `coverage-gate.yml`'s advisory→required env switch.

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] Committed coverage inventory (`reports/coverage/e2e-matrix.json` + viewer) enumerating every command, shortcut, view, plugin, route from real source.
- [x] Every command in the `GET /api/commands` catalog has a real e2e (real-server route test + scenario + Playwright + overlay component test), not a mocked-handler unit test.
- [x] Every route-declaring plugin in the §3 list has a route-level e2e booting the real handler via `tryHandleRuntimePluginRoute`/Hono dispatch.
- [x] All zero-test plugins either gain a real test or are documented test-exempt with written justification (`ZERO_TEST_EXEMPT`).
- [x] Views remain covered by the existing gates (referenced, not re-implemented per #8796/#8797/#8798).
- [x] A `check-e2e-coverage` gate fails when a new command/plugin/route ships without a real e2e; advisory standalone workflow + required `bun test` gate (baseline green).
- [x] New behavioral tests run deterministically in the PR lane (no secrets).
- [ ] Shortcuts (#8791) — gated on that registry landing; the inventory lights the surface up automatically when it does (tracked as advisory until then).

## Verification (local)
- `bun test packages/scripts/__tests__/e2e-coverage.test.ts` → 7 pass (0 blocking gaps).
- `commands-routes.real-server.test.ts` → 4 pass · `deterministic-slash-commands` scenario → pass · `slash-commands.spec.ts` → 4 pass.
- 11 plugin route tests → all pass.
- `scenario-pr-workflow.test.ts` + `deterministic-action-coverage.test.ts` meta-gates → 27 pass.

topPRs.title: sync(local-inference): eliza-1 curation onto develop — NEEDS product-direction call (curated-only vs #8808 search)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## What this is

Syncs the local-only branch `fix/local-inference-eliza1-curated` onto `develop`. It merges the latest `develop` into a snapshot of the branch and resolves the conflicts. Opened as a **draft** because it needs a product-direction call (below) and CI verification.

## ⚠️ Product-direction decision required before merge

This lands the **local-inference curation** (search disabled → curated Eliza-1 bundles only, with a dev-only `ELIZA_LOCAL_INFERENCE_ENABLE_EXTERNAL_SCAN=1` escape hatch). That **supersedes develop's just-merged user-facing generic-GGUF search (#8808) and device-tier UI (#8811)**.

A **separate, concurrent consolidation on develop kept search enabled** (develop currently has the full 422-line `hf-search.ts` + the curation's `custom-search.ts`). So the two efforts point in opposite directions for local-inference. **Maintainer must decide which wins** before this merges. Resolutions here favor the curation (search-disabled), keeping the branch internally consistent + its tests green.

## Conflict resolutions (favored the branch / curation)
- `ui` + `plugin-local-inference` `hf-search.ts`: kept the curation stub (`searchHuggingFaceGguf → []`; tests assert `[]`). develop's `runtimeClass` add is moot (no results).
- `SlotAssignments.tsx`: kept curated `assignableInstalled` rendering (matches the curation filter); removed develop's now-orphaned `runtime-class-ui` import.
- `llama.cpp` submodule: branch's #8787 fork pin (`17d1a8af` — iOS fused + Kokoro).
- `logger/package.json`, `plugin-phone/tsconfig.json`, `cloud-api/_lifecycle-fuzz.mjs`: trivial/equivalent — kept branch side (develop's vault paths preserved where additive).

Also brings the branch's non-conflicting work: voice #8785, UI-perf (WP-E/F/I), story-gate testing infra, core/message edits.

## Verification
Local `bun run verify` could not be run cleanly (isolated-worktree dep resolution; the live branch is being actively edited by a parallel agent). **Relying on this PR's CI for full typecheck/lint/test** per the repo Definition of Done.

topPRs.title: Wave 0: coding-capability audit + critical correctness fixes (workspace isolation, reload rollback) + tests
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## What this is

**Wave 0 of the coding-capability test-harness initiative** — a thorough audit of elizaOS's code-writing + agent-orchestration capability and its test/evidence coverage, plus the two critical correctness fixes and the first batch of additive tests that audit surfaced.

The audit (12 domains, 85 gaps: 8 critical / 33 high / 35 medium / 9 low) is committed under `packages/scenario-runner/docs/coding-capability-audit/` (`MASTER_PLAN.md`, `findings.json`, `PROGRESS.md`) as the actionable backlog for the remaining waves.

## Changes (each verified)

**Correctness fixes (2 critical bugs):**
- **Per-session workspace isolation** (`#audit-0.2`) — `resolveDefaultSpawnWorkdir` collapsed every route-less concurrent task into ONE shared directory, so simultaneous coding projects corrupted each other's files. Now a shared scratch root is flagged `isolate` and `spawnSession` gives each session its own `<root>/task-<id>` subdir (`computeSessionWorkdir`); cwd self-checkout and route/explicit dirs are untouched.
- **Two-phase reload rollback** (`#audit-0.3`) — `applyPluginRuntimeMutation` could unload plugins then fail mid-register, leaving the runtime half-torn-down. Now `rollbackPartialReload` restores the previous plugin graph before any restart fallback.

**Harness repair + drift guard:**
- **task-agent live E2E repaired** (`#audit-0.4`) — fixed a 3-axis breakage (missing smoke-script path, a `counter-app` mode that never existed, removed `PTYService`/`cleanForChat` imports) + a new non-live `live-smoke-imports.test.ts` that catches this class of drift in CI.
- **`@elizaos/shared` vitest resolution** (`#audit-0.1`) — the `plugin-task-coordinator` suite failed to load (`@elizaos/shared/voice-eot`), so its 18-test TaskInspector suite never ran. Fixed via source aliases.

**Additive unit coverage (6 new tests):** model-chooser contract, account-usage quota-% parsing, notify-on-completion, spawn-gate, file-lock contention, progress cadence.

## Evidence (re-verified against this PR's `develop` base in a clean worktree)

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `plugin-agent-orchestrator` unit suite | **735 passed (64 files)** |
| `plugin-task-coordinator` TaskInspector | **18 passed** (was 0 — suite failed to load) |
| `agent` plugin-runtime-apply.test | **5 passed** (pipeline previously had ZERO tests) |
| `app-core` account-usage.test | **15 passed** |
| orchestrator `typecheck` | clean |
| biome check (changed files) | clean |

**Evidence types per `PR_EVIDENCE.md`:** unit/integration test output is the appropriate proof for these correctness fixes + unit tests (attached above). Real-LLM trajectories / screenshots / video are **N/A for this PR** — they are the deferred Wave 2 ("headline live capability evidence"), which captures live coding-loop trajectories + UI recordings.

## Notes
- Authored as a clean port onto current `develop` (the original feature branch had no common ancestor with the rewritten `develop`).
- Remaining waves (evidence infra, live capability evidence, UI/surfacing/concurrency, platform breadth) are tracked in `PROGRESS.md`.
